title: Garlicky Juicy Thai (Iisan) Pork Curry
description: A flavorful and kid-friendly Thai curry dish with a garlicky taste and a hint of kombu richness, made with tender pork and fresh vegetables.
instructions: Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat. Add pork cubes and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Remove pork from the pan and set aside.
Add chopped onion to the pan and cook until softened, about 3 minutes.
Add minced garlic and cook for another minute, until fragrant.
Add Thai red curry paste and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add fish sauce, soy sauce, and grated sugarcane. Stir to combine.
Add cold-extract kombu and stir to combine.
Return pork to the pan and stir to coat with curry sauce.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer, covered, for 10 minutes, or until pork is tender.
Stir in chopped Thai basil and season with salt and black pepper.
Serve hot over steamed rice or noodles.
Garnish with additional Thai basil leaves, if desired.
Cook pork to an internal temperature of 63°C / 145°F and let it rest for 3 minutes before serving.
